source_paper_title: Analysis of the Primal-Dual Central Path for Nonlinear Semidefinite Optimization Without the Nondegeneracy Condition
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Okuno_2025, title={Analysis of the Primal-Dual Central Path for Nonlinear Semidefinite Optimization Without the Nondegeneracy Condition}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0298}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0298}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Okuno, Takayuki}, year={2025}, month=jul }
context:
Section 4 (Concluding remarks and future work), page 19.

There exist two directions for future works. The first one is concerned with limiting behavior of the tangential direction \(\dot x(\mu)\) in the x-space as \(\mu \to 0\). We have the following conjecture: \(\lim_{\mu\to 0} \dot x(\mu) = \xi^*\), where \(\xi^*\) was defined in Proposition 5.
title: Limit of the central-path tangential direction in nonlinear semidefinite optimization
background_latex:
Consider the nonlinear semidefinite optimization problem
\[\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^n} f(x) \quad \text{s.t.}\quad G(x)\in \mathbb{S}^m_+,\quad h(x)=0,\]
where \(f:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}\), \(G:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{S}^m\), and \(h:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}^s\) are twice continuously differentiable, \(\mathbb{S}^m\) denotes the space of real symmetric \(m\times m\) matrices, and \(\mathbb{S}^m_+\) (resp. \(\mathbb{S}^m_{++}\)) denotes the positive semidefinite (resp. definite) cone.

A (primal-dual) KKT triplet is \(w=(x,Y,z)\in \mathbb{R}^n\times \mathbb{S}^m\times \mathbb{R}^s\) such that
1) Stationarity: \(\nabla f(x)-JG(x)^*Y+\nabla h(x)z=0\), where \(JG(x)^*Y := [G_1(x)\bullet Y,\dots,G_n(x)\bullet Y]^\top\) and \(G_i(x)=\partial G(x)/\partial x_i\).
2) Primal/dual feasibility: \(G(x)\in\mathbb{S}^m_+\), \(Y\in\mathbb{S}^m_+\), and \(h(x)=0\).
3) Complementarity: \(G(x)\bullet Y=0\) (equivalently \(G(x)Y=0\) under \(G(x),Y\succeq 0\)).
Let \(x^*\) be a KKT point and assume conditions that guarantee a unique smooth primal-dual central path converging to \(x^*\) (in particular, strict complementarity at \(x^*\), a strong/enhanced second-order sufficient condition at \(x^*\), and Mangasarian--Fromovitz constraint qualification at \(x^*\)). Under these conditions, the paper establishes existence of a unique smooth mapping \(w(\mu)=(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))\) for \(\mu\in(0,\bar\mu)\) such that \(w(\mu)\in \mathbb{R}^n\times\mathbb{S}^m_{++}\times\mathbb{R}^s\) satisfies the barrier KKT (BKKT) system
\[\nabla f(x(\mu)) - JG(x(\mu))^*Y(\mu) + \nabla h(x(\mu))z(\mu)=0,\quad h(x(\mu))=0,\quad G(x(\mu))Y(\mu)=\mu I.\]
This curve is the (primal-dual) central path.

The paper also identifies a specific direction \(\xi^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) characterized as the unique \(\Delta x\)-component (when it exists) of solutions \((\Delta x,\Delta Y)\) to a certain linear equation system obtained by formally differentiating a symmetrized BKKT system at the limit KKT triplet \(w_a=(x^*,Y_a,z_a)\), where \((Y_a,z_a)\) is the analytic center of the Lagrange multiplier set at \(x^*\). Concretely, \(\xi^*\) is the unique \(\Delta x\)-component of solutions to the paper's system (3.18)--(3.20), and the central path satisfies the first-order expansion \(x(\mu)=x^*+\mu\xi^*+o(\mu)\) as \(\mu\downarrow 0\).

For the smooth central path, define the tangential (derivative) direction in the primal space by
\[\dot x(\mu) := \frac{d}{d\mu} x(\mu),\qquad \mu\in(0,\bar\mu).\]
problem_definition_latex:
Under the assumptions that guarantee existence and uniqueness of a smooth central path \(w(\mu)=(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))\) for a nonlinear semidefinite program and the associated direction \(\xi^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) defined as the unique \(\Delta x\)-component of solutions to the paper's linearized system at the limiting KKT triplet \(w_a=(x^*,Y_a,z_a)\), determine whether the following limit holds:
\[\lim_{\mu\to 0} \dot x(\mu) = \xi^*.\]

location_in_paper: Section 4 (Concluding remarks and future work), page 19.
open_problem_quote: There exist two directions for future works. The first one is concerned with limiting behavior of the tangential direction \(\dot x(\mu)\) in the x-space as \(\mu \to 0\). We have the following conjecture: \(\lim_{\mu\to 0} \dot x(\mu) = \xi^*\), where \(\xi^*\) was defined in Proposition 5.
subject_classification: Nonlinear semidefinite programming; interior-point methods; sensitivity/central-path analysis
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The conjecture concerns a limiting derivative along an implicitly defined smooth path near a KKT point under fairly technical regularity assumptions, so falsifying it would likely require constructing delicate smooth NSDP instances and verifying the assumptions plus computing the central path derivative accurately.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Automatically evaluating candidate proofs or disproofs is not straightforward: one would need symbolic/analytic control of the central path and its derivative under general smooth data, and there is no simple computable fitness function that reliably reflects progress toward establishing the limit.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
